sourceMovie.title: Ice Age: Collision Course
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Ice Age: Collision Course for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a animation rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Mike Thurmeier keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Shaun the Sheep Movie: Farmageddon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ice Age: Collision Course clicked for you, A Shaun the Sheep Movie: Farmageddon (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ice Age: Collision Course?
faq.answer: Try A Shaun the Sheep Movie: Farmageddon, Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa—each shares DNA with Ice Age: Collision Course while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ice Age: Collision Course?
faq.answer: Ice Age: Collision Course (2016) is best described as Adventure, Animation, Family, Comedy, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ice Age: Collision Course part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ice Age Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
